steps.title: Find undergraduate students from the graduate-to-undergraduate relationship
steps.content:
Graduate students represent 35% of undergraduate students. With 4,851 graduate students surveyed:
$$4,851 = 0.35 \times U$$
Divide both sides by 0.35:
$$U = \frac{4,851}{0.35} = 13,860$$

steps.title: Find postdoctoral students from the undergraduate-to-postdoctoral relationship
steps.content:
Undergraduate students represent 6600% of postdoctoral students. Converting 6600% to decimal form: 6600% = 66. Using U = 13,860:
$$13,860 = 66 \times P$$
Divide both sides by 66:
$$P = \frac{13,860}{66} = 210$$

steps.title: Add the two student populations
steps.content:
$$13,860 + 210 = 14,070$$
The sum of undergraduate and postdoctoral students surveyed is 14,070.
